flavor_text: A frayed hood that smells of candle wax and dust.
This veil was stitched by an apprentice with shaking hands.
Plain cloth gathers soot from every failed spark.
Its edge is singed where a cantrip misfired.
Worn wrap stitched over symbols no one admits drawing.
A runed hood for hedge rites in leaking chapels.
Thin wards crawl the hem like restless ants.
This cowl quiets breath before uncertain invocations.
The brow knot holds a shard of grave glass.
Field veil woven to keep ash from open eyes.
Old sigils fade, then return during new moons.
A scribed hood favored by exiled tutors.
Its inner weave hums when nearby prayers go wrong.
Bound cloth dulls torchlight around the face.
Worn by circle adepts who worked without witnesses.
The mask thread was spun from funeral linen.
A sigiled cowl that steadies trembling focus.
Ember marks lace the rim from wardfire.
This hood hides the stare of one who has seen too much.
Nocturne veil carried into rites beneath drowned bells.
The seam knots resemble tiny hanging men.
A highrite cowl for declarations of forbidden law.
Its shadow clings even at noon.
Veteran magi kept this hood for final castings.
An ascendant veil woven for liturgies that silence cities.
A plain robe patched with thread the color of old ash.

Frayed cloth thins where spellbooks rode against the chest.

A focus mantle for mages who barter in favors.

Scribed vestment worn in plague prayers and bone rites.

Warded cloth swallows sparks before they find flesh.

A sigiled raiment stitched with names of dead mentors.

Nocturne robes for vigils in shuttered cathedrals.
The weave tightens like muscle when danger nears.
Highrite raiment reserved for verdict spells.
It hangs like mourning cloth over a moving grave.
Veteran arcanists called this cut the black sermon.
Ascendant vestment meant for thrones of dust and lightning.
Simple shoulder wraps knotted with black thread.
One drape sags where salt rain ate the seam.
Frayed mantle cloth cushions the clavicle from recoil.
Plain wraps whisper when the wearer turns too fast.
Rough drapes hide stitchwork over ritual scars.
Runed shoulderwraps for long hours over cold braziers.
The left fold bears chalk from broken circles.
Bound mantles keep sleeves clear of candlefire.
A focus drape favored by crypt readers.

Scribed mantles worn by ward-chanters at city gates.

Warded folds catch drifting embers and dead moths.
Worn by adepts who watched plague pyres all winter.
Sigiled shouldercloth stitched with silver funeral wire.
The drape edge crackles in storm air.
Shadow wraps darken as casting strain rises.
Each fold remembers a vow spoken in fear.
Nocturne mantles for rites beneath eclipsed glass.

Highrite drapes mark rank among unkind scholars.
They fall like altar cloth on a butcher block.
Veterans kept these wraps for banishment nights.
Ascendant mantles made for ceremonies that end dynasties.
Frayed gloves with thin padding over burnt knuckles.
These handwraps smell of copper and lamp oil.
Plain cuffs hide ink stains and tremor lines.
Rough cloth leaves the fingertips bare for sigils.
Worn mitts were mended with pages from a torn primer.
Runed gloves steady chalk lines on shaking stone.

Bound cuffs keep sleeves from dragging through circles.
A focus pair worn by reluctant hexbreakers.

Ascendant handwraps for spells that leave no witness.
A plain sash tied twice against slipping robes.

Frayed knots hold pouches of chalk and grave salt.
This belt creaks softly under glass vials.
Worn loops hang with charms that no longer answer.
Runed cincture for carrying seals and bone needles.
The buckle is etched with a half-erased ward.

A focus belt worn by corridor duelists.
